title: The Shining
description: Jack Torrance takes a job as winter caretaker of the Overlook Hotel, bringing his wife Wendy and young son Danny. Isolated by snow and stalked by something that knows Jack's every weakness, the family slowly comes apart. Based on the novel by Stephen King and the 1980 film directed by Stanley Kubrick.
setup: Build or download a large, labyrinthine hotel lot — many corridors, ornate rooms, a grand ballroom, and a hedge maze if possible. Moonwood Mill or a large Windenburg lot works well.
The caretaker's quarters must be separated from the hotel's public rooms — Jack's office, a typewriter, and a bar are essential.

Jack Torrance is a middle-aged adult — a writer in recovery, brilliant and brittle. Suggested traits: Creative, Bookworm, Hot-Headed, Erratic, Mean. He must begin the scenario with zero Wellness skill and no bar-related objects in his quarters.
Wendy Torrance is a young adult — practical, loving, and far stronger than she appears. Suggested traits: Family-Oriented, Neat, Brave (use Active), Romantic, Gloomy.
Danny Torrance is a child Sim — unusually perceptive, sensitive, and frightened of Room 217. Suggested traits: Genius, Bookworm, Loner.
The hotel bar must be locked for the first 14 days. When it unlocks, Jack gains free access. Track his bar visits daily.
Room 217 (or 237) must be locked for the entire scenario. It may be furnished however you choose — but no Sim should enter it unless triggering an event.
goals: Jack must complete a manuscript — reach Writing level 5 — before the hotel bar unlocks. If he does not, his deterioration accelerates.
Jack's relationship with Wendy must decay from Good Friends to Enemies status.
Danny must retain green mood throughout — the scenario ends early if he is harmed.
rules: Every day: Jack must spend at least 3 hours writing. If he does not, his Erratic trait triggers — he must perform one mean or mischievous interaction that day.
Every day from Day 14 onward: Jack may visit the bar. Each visit costs one positive relationship point with Wendy.
Every day: Danny must spend time alone in a part of the hotel away from Jack.

Once per week: a 'hotel event' occurs — choose from: a mysterious locked door opens, a social interaction appears between Jack and an invisible guest, a room fills with water, or a piece of furniture moves.
If Jack's mood is red for 2 consecutive days: he enters a Rage state. He must perform mean interactions with every household member before his mood can recover.
Wendy may not leave the lot without Danny. If Danny's mood drops to red, Wendy immediately attempts to organise departure.

storyBeats.text: The Torrance family arrives at the Overlook as the last guests depart. The hotel is magnificent and enormous and entirely theirs.
Jack writes. The manuscript grows slowly. The hotel is quiet. Danny explores its corridors and avoids Room 217 for reasons he cannot explain.
The snow comes. The road closes. The hotel gets louder — or seems to. Jack hears things in the ballroom.
The bar unlocks. Jack tells himself one drink won't hurt. Wendy watches his eyes when he comes back to their quarters.
Jack's manuscript is abandoned. The pages he reads back to himself are not what he thought he wrote.
The hotel has Jack now. Danny calls for help. The hedge maze is the last place any of them want to be.

endings.title: REDRUM
endings.text: Jack's relationship with Wendy reaches Enemies. He enters a Rage state and pursues Wendy and Danny across the lot. If Wendy and Danny escape the lot by any means — taxi, door — while Jack is confined to the hotel wing: they survive. Jack remains. He has always been the caretaker.

endings.title: The Manuscript
endings.text: Jack reaches Writing level 5 before Day 14 and does not visit the bar at all. His mood stabilises. He and Wendy reach Soulmate status. They leave the Overlook at season's end. The hotel stands empty again, waiting for the next caretaker.

endings.title: All Work and No Play
endings.text: Jack's manuscript is never finished. His mood remains red. He never leaves the bar wing. Wendy and Danny depart on Day 21 without him. Jack stays behind. The scenario ends when he reaches Enemies status with every household member. He is home.

title: Rebecca
description: A shy, unnamed young woman marries the brooding widower Maxim de Winter and is brought to Manderley, his grand estate — only to find the house still entirely in thrall to his dead first wife, Rebecca. The housekeeper Mrs Danvers worships Rebecca's memory and will do anything to protect it. Based on the novel by Daphne du Maurier and the 1940 Hitchcock film.
setup: Build or download a large, grand manor lot — multiple storeys, formal gardens, a boathouse if possible. Henford-on-Bagley or Tartosa suits the mood.
A sealed-off wing or locked upstairs bedroom represents Rebecca's rooms — untouched, preserved. This room must never be redecorated.

The Second Mrs de Winter is a young adult — low funds of her own, recently married. Suggested traits: Shy, Bookworm, Romantic, Neat, Insecure (use Gloomy as a substitute). She begins with no friends on the lot.
Maxim de Winter is a middle-aged adult Sim — wealthy, reserved, with a hidden dark past. Suggested traits: Snob, Loner, Romantic, Gloomy, Proper (use Neat).

Mrs Danvers is a household member or frequent visitor — older adult, immaculately dressed in black. Suggested traits: Mean, Jealous, Gloomy, Perfectionist, Loner. She has max Charisma and Mischief skill.
Scatter portraits, monogrammed items (renamed via debug), and expensive furnishings associated with Rebecca throughout the house. Rebecca's rooms should be the most lavishly decorated on the lot.
The Second Mrs de Winter begins with no Cooking, Charisma, or Flower Arranging skill — she must earn her place at Manderley.
goals: The Second Mrs de Winter must reach level 5 in Charisma and host one successful dinner party (all guests' needs green at end of event).
Maxim and the Second Mrs de Winter must reach Soulmate status.
Mrs Danvers must be removed from the household — by being fired (evicted), breaking down (prolonged red mood), or through a fire event.
rules: Every day: Mrs Danvers performs one act of sabotage — autonomously or player-directed. Options include: breaking an appliance, sending a mean social interaction to the Second Mrs de Winter, hiding food, or criticising her outfit.
Every day: the Second Mrs de Winter must greet at least one NPC or household member. She may not initiate romantic interactions with Maxim until Charisma level 3.
Rebecca's rooms must remain locked to the Second Mrs de Winter for the first 14 in-game days.
Every 7 days: host a social event at Manderley. If the event rating is below Silver, Mrs Danvers gains a confidence boost — add one new mean interaction she is permitted to use.
If Maxim's mood ever drops to red: he must withdraw from the household socially for 24 in-game hours. No interactions initiated by him.
Once the Second Mrs de Winter discovers Rebecca's rooms (Day 14): she may redecorate one item. Mrs Danvers immediately breaks something in retaliation.

storyBeats.text: The new Mrs de Winter arrives at Manderley for the first time. Mrs Danvers greets her at the door — correct, composed, and entirely unwelcoming.
The house is full of Rebecca. Her monogram is on the stationery, her portrait dominates the hall, and Mrs Danvers speaks of her at every opportunity.
The Second Mrs de Winter attempts to establish herself — hosting guests, managing the household, reaching toward Maxim. Mrs Danvers undermines her at every turn.
Rebecca's rooms are finally unlocked. The Second Mrs de Winter steps inside for the first time and understands the full weight of what she is competing with.
Maxim's secret begins to surface. The relationship deepens — or fractures — depending on what comes to light.
A confrontation with Mrs Danvers. Manderley's fate, and theirs, hangs in the balance.

endings.title: Manderley Burns
endings.text: Mrs Danvers's mood is critically red and she has been refused eviction. Set a fire on the lot while she is inside Rebecca's rooms. If she perishes, the Second Mrs de Winter and Maxim escape and begin a new life. If she survives, she is evicted in the chaos. Manderley is sold.

endings.title: The Mistress of Manderley
endings.text: The Second Mrs de Winter reaches Charisma 5, hosts a Gold-rated dinner party, and reaches Soulmate status with Maxim. Mrs Danvers is evicted. The house is slowly, carefully redecorated. One room at a time.

endings.title: Rebecca Wins
endings.text: The Second Mrs de Winter's mood remains red for 7 consecutive days and she and Maxim never reach Good Friends status. She leaves the household. Mrs Danvers locks Rebecca's rooms again. Nothing at Manderley changes.

title: Misery
description: Celebrated novelist Paul Sheldon crashes his car in a blizzard and is rescued by Annie Wilkes — his self-proclaimed number one fan. What begins as recovery becomes imprisonment as Annie's adoration curdles into something far more dangerous. Based on the novel by Stephen King and the 1990 film starring Kathy Bates and James Caan.
setup: Use a remote lot in a snowy or rural world — Henford-on-Bagley or a custom mountain build works well. The house should be isolated with no nearby neighbours.
Paul's bedroom must be locked from the outside. It should contain a bed, a desk with a typewriter or computer, and basic need-satisfying items — but no phone.

Annie is a middle-aged adult Sim, large household funds, unemployed or retired. Suggested traits: Erratic, Mean, Perfectionist, Loner, Gloomy. Give her a garden and cooking hobby to reflect her domestic facade.

Paul is a young or middle-aged adult with high Creativity skill. Suggested traits: Bookworm, Creative, Neat, Romantic. He begins the scenario injured — remove his ability to leave the lot via locked doors.
Stock Paul's room with writing materials only. He has no access to the kitchen, garden, or front door without Annie's presence.
Annie's house should feel warm and obsessive — bookshelves full of Paul's 'novels', fan memorabilia, a large kitchen where she cooks his meals, and a pristine garden.
goals: Paul must complete a full manuscript — reach level 10 Writing skill or finish a book using the in-game writing system.
Annie's mood must remain consistently green for 14 consecutive days — she is content with Paul's progress and behaviour.
rules: Every day: Annie brings Paul his meals. Paul may not access the kitchen independently.
Every day: Annie checks Paul's writing progress. If no progress was made that day, remove one comfort item from the room.
Every day: Annie and Paul must have at least one conversation. Annie may be warm or hostile depending on her current mood.
If Paul attempts to leave the locked room (use the door to test): trigger a punishment event. Remove a room item and lock the room for an additional in-game day with no visits.
Every 7 days: Annie reads Paul's latest chapter. If her mood is green, she adds a new item to Paul's room. If red, she enters a rage — break or remove an item of your choosing.
Paul may attempt an escape once per week during Annie's sleep cycle. If caught, apply the punishment rule.

storyBeats.text: Paul wakes in Annie's spare room, legs immobilised. She is attentive, cheerful, and certain she has saved his life.
Annie reads Paul's newest manuscript and is horrified by what he has done to her favourite character. The warm facade cracks.
Paul realises the doors are locked and the phone is gone. He begins exploring the house during Annie's supply runs.
Annie discovers his snooping. A punishment follows. Paul resolves to be more careful — and more convincing.
The manuscript grows. Paul begins writing strategically — keeping Annie placated, buying himself time.
A final confrontation approaches. The manuscript is nearly done. Only one of them may walk away from what comes next.

endings.title: The Manuscript
endings.text: Paul completes the book and Annie's mood is green for 14 consecutive days. She releases him, believing their bond is real. Paul escapes and Annie is left alone with her garden, her shelves, and her certainty that she was his number one fan.

endings.title: The Hobbling
endings.text: Paul attempts escape and is caught. Annie's rage peaks — she destroys all his writing materials except the manuscript. Paul begins again from scratch, more broken than before. The scenario resets from Story Beat 4.

endings.title: The Fire
endings.text: Paul sets the kitchen alight during a confrontation. Both Sims are placed in the burning room. Whoever survives inherits the lot and the scenario ends. If Annie survives, she begins again with a new captive.

title: The Collector
description: A lonely, obsessive man wins the lottery and uses his newfound wealth to capture Miranda, the art student he has fixated on from afar. He wants her to come to love him — she wants to survive and escape. Based on the novel by John Fowles and the 1965 film adaptation starring Samantha Eggar.
setup: Use a countryside lot — Henford-on-Bagley is ideal. A large isolated house (e.g. a beachhouse in Tartosa) also works.
A cellar is required, either pre-existing or built in. Add a fake door or grass covering to hide its existence from any passing Sims.

The Collector is a wealthy young adult Sim — large household funds, unemployed, no friends. Suggested traits: Mean, Antisocial, Hates Children, Unromantic, Gloomy. Bonus: give them the Butterfly Collector hobby if you have Adventure Awaits.

The Victim is also a young adult. Appearance, gender, and personality are entirely your choice. You may create a custom Sim resembling Miranda Grey, or have the Collector stalk and 'kidnap' an existing townie.
The cellar must satisfy all the Victim's needs except two: no fridge (food is brought by the Collector) and no bath (the Victim may use a bath elsewhere in the house on supervised trips only).
Stock the cellar with items matching the Victim's interests — art supplies and canvases for a creative Sim, instruments or a stereo for a musical one.
The cellar door must remain locked at all times except for supervised bath trips and the weekly fancy dinner.
goals: The Victim's mood must be consistently green — all needs met, no prolonged distress.
The Victim and Collector must reach Good Friends status, or show mutual romantic interest (pink indicators on both sides).
rules: Every day: the Collector visits the cellar, brings food, disposes of rubbish, and does laundry (if you have the pack).
Every day: the Collector has at least one conversation with the Victim — topic is your choice.
Every day: the Collector watches the Victim perform one activity (drawing, dancing, gaming, etc.).
Whenever a Victim need drops to red: trigger an escape attempt — remove one hobby item from the cellar as punishment.
Every day with no escape attempt: add one new hobby item of your choosing to the cellar.
Every day: the Victim takes a supervised bath trip. You may use this as a second opportunity for an escape attempt.

Every 7 days: hold a fancy dinner in the main house. Both Sims dress up. The Collector may attempt romantic interactions, propose if available, or WooHoo if desired. The Victim returns to the cellar when dinner ends.

storyBeats.text: Frederick prepares the countryside house — builds or fits out the cellar, installs the hidden door, and stocks it for his Victim's interests.
The Victim arrives. They test every corner of the cellar, looking for weakness. Frederick watches through the locked door.
A routine establishes itself. Daily visits, small gifts, careful conversation. Frederick believes things are improving.
The first red need triggers an escape attempt. Frederick removes a hobby item. The cellar feels smaller.
The first fancy dinner. Both dress up. Frederick attempts to romance or connect. The Victim weighs their options across the candlelit table.
The loop continues — new victims if needed — until the two conditions are finally met, or one of them doesn't make it.

endings.title: The Reform
endings.text: The Victim's mood is consistently green. The two have reached Good Friends status or show mutual romantic interest from both sides. The Victim is released to the main house as a roommate or platonic spouse. Frederick reforms — he never collects again.

endings.title: The Punishment
endings.text: The Victim's needs drop critically — loneliness, or a red moodlet from a Social Butterfly trait. They attempt escape and are caught. Frederick punishes them by your method of choice. The cellar is repurposed for a new Victim. Begin again.

endings.title: The Accident
endings.text: The Victim deteriorates while the Collector is away — starvation, illness, hysteria, or another fate of your choosing. This mirrors the novel's ending. Frederick, unmoved, clears the cellar and selects a new Victim. Begin again.

title: The Haunting of Hill House
description: Dr Montague invites a small group to spend the summer at Hill House, a mansion with a long history of madness and death. As the weeks pass, the house begins to single out Eleanor — the most psychologically fragile of the guests — drawing her inward until she can no longer tell where she ends and the house begins. Based on the novel by Shirley Jackson and the 2018 Netflix series.
setup: Build or download a large, asymmetrical, unsettling manor. Every room should feel slightly wrong — mismatched angles, too-dark corners, furniture that doesn't quite fit. Windenburg or Moonwood Mill are recommended worlds.
The house must have at least 6 rooms plus a cellar. One room — the Cold Room — must be furnished identically to Eleanor's childhood bedroom, down to the wallpaper. This room unlocks at Day 14.

Eleanor Vance is the protagonist — young adult, recently freed from a long caregiving stint. Suggested traits: Gloomy, Loner, Paranoid (use Erratic), Bookworm, Romantic. She begins the scenario with no friends and low household funds.
Dr Montague is an older adult academic — curious, well-meaning, increasingly out of his depth. Suggested traits: Bookworm, Genius, Proper, Oblivious (use Cheerful as ironic contrast).
Theodora is Eleanor's foil — confident, sociable, slightly cruel. Suggested traits: Outgoing, Mischievous, Creative, Self-Assured.
Luke Sanderson is the heir to Hill House — charming, unreliable, and secretly frightened. Suggested traits: Charming (use Romantic), Lazy, Kleptomaniac, Gloomy.
Add 1–2 additional household members or regular visitors to represent the research group. The house must never feel fully safe or fully explained.
goals: Eleanor must reach level 3 Friendship with every other household member — and then lose it. All friendships must decay back below Friend status before the ending triggers.
Eleanor must spend 3 consecutive in-game days with a red Social need and no successful friendly interactions.
The Cold Room must be discovered by Eleanor on or after Day 14.
rules: Every day: Eleanor must spend at least 2 hours alone in a room not occupied by any other Sim.

Every day: roll a die or use a random trigger. On a 1 or 2, a 'haunting event' occurs — choose from: all lights on the lot flicker off for one in-game hour, a door locks or unlocks without explanation, Eleanor's hunger drops two bars instantly, or an object breaks.
Every day: Theodora must perform one socially cutting interaction toward Eleanor — teasing, dismissing, or excluding.
If Eleanor's mood is red for 2 consecutive days: she enters an Absorbed state. She may only interact with the house itself (objects, environment) for the next 24 hours — no social interactions.
The Cold Room remains locked until Day 14. When unlocked, Eleanor must spend her first waking hours of that day inside it alone.
No Sim may leave the lot for the first 21 days. Dr Montague may make one off-lot trip per week for supplies.

storyBeats.text: The group arrives at Hill House. It is larger than expected and strangely quiet. Everyone agrees it has a peculiar atmosphere. No one agrees on what kind.
The first haunting event. Something moves or changes without explanation. Dr Montague takes notes. Eleanor feels, privately, that it was meant for her.
Friendships form — Eleanor and Theodora are briefly, warmly close. The house seems to ease. This does not last.
Theodora pulls away. The hauntings intensify and become more personal. Eleanor finds writing on the wall that uses her name.
The Cold Room unlocks. Eleanor steps inside and recognises it. She does not tell the others what she felt there.
Eleanor can no longer be sure what is the house and what is herself. The others begin to talk about leaving. Eleanor does not want to go.

endings.title: Journey's End
endings.text: Eleanor's three friendship goals are met and then lost. Her Social need is red for 3 consecutive days. On Day 21 or later, when the others attempt to leave: Eleanor's Sim is directed to the car — but you cancel the action at the last moment. She remains. She is home. Remove Eleanor from the household. The others depart. Hill House stands empty.

endings.title: The Rescue
endings.text: Dr Montague's mood remains green throughout and he retains Friend status with Eleanor to the end. He intervenes before the ending triggers — Eleanor is removed from the lot by taxi. She survives, changed. The research group never returns to Hill House.

endings.title: The Record
endings.text: All four Sims survive to Day 28. Dr Montague completes his research notes (finish a book on the Writing skill). The group departs. Hill House is left exactly as it was. Whatever lived there is still living there.

title: Rosemary's Baby
description: Rosemary and Guy Woodhouse move into the Bramford, a prestigious Manhattan apartment building with a dark history. Their neighbours — the Castevets — are warm, eccentric, and entirely too interested in Rosemary's wellbeing. As her pregnancy progresses, Rosemary becomes increasingly certain that something is very wrong — with her body, her husband, and everyone around her. Based on the novel by Ira Levin and the 1968 film directed by Roman Polanski.
setup: Use an apartment lot or a large multi-unit build in a city world — San Myshuno is ideal. The Woodhouse apartment should be comfortable but modestly furnished.
The Castevet apartment must share a wall with the Woodhouses. It should be cluttered with herbs, occult objects, and overly personal decor.
Rosemary is a young adult — warm, sociable, and recently married. Suggested traits: Romantic, Family-Oriented, Outgoing, Neat, Cheerful. She begins the scenario with no pregnancy.
Guy Woodhouse is an ambitious young adult actor — charming on the surface, hollow beneath. Suggested traits: Romantic, Self-Assured, Ambitious, Jealous, Snob.

Minnie Castevet is a loud, intrusive older adult neighbour — impossible to dislike on the surface. Suggested traits: Outgoing, Nosy (use Mischievous), Cheerful, Mean. She has high Cooking skill and always arrives with food.
Roman Castevet is Minnie's husband — quiet, watchful, distinguished. Suggested traits: Genius, Snob, Loner, Gloomy.
Rosemary must become pregnant by Day 7. From that point, her autonomy begins to narrow — track her needs carefully and apply restrictions weekly.
goals: Rosemary must carry a pregnancy to term while her Friendship score with every non-household Sim decays to zero.
Guy must reach Soulmate status with either Minnie or Roman — representing his complicity.
Rosemary must spend the final 7 days of pregnancy unable to leave the apartment lot.
rules: Every day: Minnie visits and brings a meal or drink. Rosemary must consume it before Minnie leaves.
Every day from Week 2 onward: Rosemary loses access to one outside friendship. Use the relationship panel to gradually decay non-household relationships.
Every day: Guy must perform one social interaction with the Castevets before interacting with Rosemary.
Once per week: Rosemary is permitted one off-lot outing without Guy. If her mood drops to red during this outing, she must return immediately.
From Week 3 onward: Rosemary may not call, text, or invite any Sim outside the Castevet circle. Lock her phone interactions if possible via mods, or use the honour system.
Any time Rosemary's Comfort or Social drops to red: Guy and Minnie must immediately attend to her — but only in ways that serve their goal, not hers.

storyBeats.text: Rosemary and Guy settle into the Bramford. The apartment is perfect. The Castevets next door are a little much — but warm, and generous, and always there.
Rosemary falls pregnant. Everyone is delighted. Minnie begins bringing her daily drinks — tannis root, she says. Good for the baby.
Rosemary's old friendships begin to fade. Her calls go unreturned. Guy is busy, distracted, and oddly cheerful for a man whose wife is suffering.
The pregnancy is difficult. Rosemary loses weight. The doctor Minnie recommends dismisses her concerns. Guy agrees with the doctor.
Rosemary begins to piece it together — too slowly, too late. She reaches out to an old friend. He dies before he can help her.
The baby is coming. Rosemary is not allowed to leave.

endings.title: All of Them Witches
endings.text: Rosemary carries the pregnancy to term. She has no friendships outside the apartment. She is shown the baby and, after a moment of horror, begins to rock the cradle. The scenario ends. Guy and the Castevets have won. The baby will be raised in the Bramford.

endings.title: The Escape
endings.text: If Rosemary retains at least one outside friendship at full green and her mood is green for 3 consecutive days in Week 5: she escapes the lot before the birth. Move her to a new apartment alone. She raises the child. She never sees Guy or the Castevets again.

endings.title: The Doubt
endings.text: Rosemary's mood is consistently red through the final trimester. She gives birth, sees the baby, and does nothing. She neither rocks the cradle nor flees. She sits in the room and the scenario simply ends, unresolved — which is the most honest ending of all.

title: Twin Peaks
description: The body of homecoming queen Laura Palmer is found wrapped in plastic on the shore of a small Pacific Northwest logging town. FBI Special Agent Dale Cooper arrives to investigate, drawn by something in Twin Peaks that goes deeper than murder — a darkness that lives in the woods, speaks through dreams, and has been feeding on the town for longer than anyone wants to admit. Based on the television series created by David Lynch and Mark Frost.
setup: Use a large, dense woodland world — Moonwood Mill is ideal, or a custom Henford-on-Bagley build with heavy tree coverage and a body of water at the lot boundary. The town must feel both cosy and wrong.

Build or place several distinct structures on or adjacent to the main lot: the Double R Diner, the sheriff's station (a large open-plan build), the Great Northern Hotel (the grandest residential lot available), and a roadside cabin in the trees representing the Palmer house.

A single locked room in the largest structure represents the Black Lodge — curtained in red, chevron-patterned floor if possible using debug tiles, containing only two chairs and a lamp. This room unlocks at Day 21.

Laura Palmer does not begin the scenario as a playable Sim. She is represented by a locked memorial object — a framed photo or candle arrangement — placed at the water's edge on Day 1. She may appear as a ghost from Day 7 onward.

Dale Cooper is a young or middle-aged adult — meticulous, joyful, and entirely serious about both. Suggested traits: Genius, Foodie, Outgoing, Bookworm, Good. He arrives on the lot on Day 1 with a single suitcase and immediately records a message into a handheld object representing his tape recorder.
Sheriff Harry S. Truman is a middle-aged adult — decent, loyal, out of his depth in the best possible way. Suggested traits: Good, Neat, Family-Oriented, Loner, Brave (use Active).
Audrey Horne is a young adult — the sharpest person in any room she enters, bored enough to be dangerous. Suggested traits: Mischievous, Self-Assured, Romantic, Genius, Outgoing.
Bobby Briggs is a young adult — loud, grieving, involved in things he cannot talk about. Suggested traits: Hot-Headed, Romantic, Lazy, Mean.
Donna Hayward is a young adult — Laura's best friend, already pulling at threads she was warned to leave alone. Suggested traits: Romantic, Bookworm, Good, Outgoing.
Benjamin Horne is a middle-aged adult — the wealthiest man in town, charming and entirely corrupt. Suggested traits: Snob, Ambitious, Romantic, Mischievous, Mean.

Leland Palmer is a middle-aged adult — Laura's father, a grieving wreck with something living behind his eyes. Suggested traits: Erratic, Gloomy, Romantic, Hot-Headed. He must share a household with his wife Sarah.
Sarah Palmer is a middle-aged adult — medicated, visionary, already knows more than she can say. Suggested traits: Erratic, Gloomy, Loner, Bookworm.

Bob is not a playable Sim. He is represented by a hidden object — a debug item placed in Leland's bedroom and never moved — that only Cooper may interact with. Interacting with it counts as a clue discovery.

The town must have at least 8 NPCs representing townspeople — the Log Lady, Big Ed, Norma, Nadine, James, Leo Johnson, Josie Packard, and Pete Martell. These may be household visitors or ambient NPCs. Each must have one distinctive trait that sets them apart.
goals: Cooper must discover 7 clues before Day 21 — each represented by completing a 2-hour research or writing task, or by interacting with a specific object or Sim designated as a clue source at the start of the scenario.
Cooper must reach Good Friends status with at least 5 other household or NPC Sims — the town must trust him.
Leland's secret must be uncovered before Day 28 — represented by Cooper interacting with the Bob object in Leland's bedroom while Leland is present on the lot.
Cooper must enter the Black Lodge on Day 21 or later — he must spend at least one in-game hour alone in the red room.
The Double R Diner must remain open — at least one meal must be served there every day. If it closes for a full day, a bad omen triggers.
rules: Every morning: Cooper records a tape — represented by a 30-minute solo activity at his desk before interacting with any other Sim. If he skips this, he loses one clue progress that day.
Every morning: Cooper must eat pie and drink coffee at the Double R Diner before beginning his investigation. Both needs — Hunger and Social — must be green before he leaves the diner lot.
Every day: Cooper and Harry must meet for at least one joint conversation or activity. They are partners. The investigation does not proceed without both of them.
Every day: one NPC townsperson must approach Cooper unsolicited — represented by the player choosing a random NPC to initiate conversation with him. What they tell him counts as ambient clue colour.
Every day: Leland must perform one erratic or hostile interaction — crying at an object, dancing alone, or arguing with Sarah. This is not optional. Something is wearing him.
Every day: Audrey must perform one act of independent investigation — going somewhere she was told not to, talking to someone she was told to avoid, or accessing a locked space.
Every day: the Log Lady NPC must visit the lot once and deliver a cryptic statement — represented by initiating a conversation with Cooper and then immediately leaving.
Once per week: a dream sequence occurs. Move Cooper to the Black Lodge room alone for one in-game hour. When he exits, he gains one clue automatically — the dream told him something.
Once per week: hold a town gathering at the Double R Diner — all available Sims on the lot, green moods encouraged. The town is trying to hold itself together.
If Sarah's mood drops to red for 2 consecutive days: she has a vision. She must spend 4 hours alone and may not be interacted with. When she emerges, Cooper gains half a clue.
Bobby and Donna may not be in the same room without a hostile interaction triggering between them. They are grieving differently and it keeps coming out wrong.
Benjamin Horne must attempt one manipulation per week — a mean, mischievous, or romantic interaction directed at a Sim who is not his spouse.
The Bob object in Leland's bedroom may only be interacted with by Cooper. If any other Sim touches it: Leland's erratic trait triggers immediately and he must perform a hostile interaction.

storyBeats.text: Cooper arrives in Twin Peaks on a Tuesday morning. The coffee is extraordinary. The body was found wrapped in plastic at the water's edge. He records everything into his tape recorder and begins to fall in love with the town before he has even met it.
The town assembles around the investigation — some offering too much, some offering nothing. Audrey watches Cooper from across the diner. The Log Lady has been waiting for him.
The first dream. Cooper wakes with partial knowledge he cannot fully articulate. He writes it down anyway. Harry watches him with patient confusion.
Donna and James begin pulling at Laura's secrets. What they find is not what they expected. Bobby gets drunk and says something true by accident.
Leland dances. Sarah sees something in the corner of the living room that is not there when anyone else looks. The Bob object vibrates with proximity.
Cooper reaches the seventh clue. The pattern assembles. Leland is in the room. The Bob object is no longer just an object.
Day 21. The Black Lodge unlocks. Cooper has been here before — in the dream, in the backward speech, in the place between the trees. He goes in anyway.

endings.title: The Arm
endings.text: Cooper discovers all 7 clues, enters the Black Lodge on Day 21, and spends one hour inside alone. When he exits, Leland's secret is exposed — represented by a final confrontation scene in the Palmer house. Leland is removed from the household. The case is closed. Cooper sits in the Double R Diner at dawn and orders pie. Something is slightly wrong with his reflection but he does not mention it. The town goes on.

endings.title: Fire Walk With Me
endings.text: Cooper discovers fewer than 5 clues before Day 21. He enters the Black Lodge underprepared. He does not come out the same way he went in — represent this by changing one of his traits to Erratic permanently. The investigation continues without resolution. Audrey keeps looking. Donna stops. The woods are quiet in a way that means something is satisfied.

endings.title: The Mystery Remains
endings.text: Cooper never enters the Black Lodge. He solves the surface case — Leland is exposed — but the deeper thing is never addressed. He is reassigned. He drives out of Twin Peaks on a grey morning with excellent coffee and an incomplete tape. The red room stays locked. Laura's photo stays at the water's edge. Whatever lives in the woods waits for the next person who gets close enough to hear it.

title: Dexter
description: Dexter Morgan is a blood spatter analyst for the Miami Metro Police Department. He is also a serial killer. He loves his sister, his colleagues, his routine. He follows the Code his adoptive father Harry drilled into him — only kill those who deserve it, never get caught, keep the mask on. The mask is the work. Based on the Showtime series developed by James Manos Jr. and the novels by Jeff Lindsay.
setup: Use a city world — San Myshuno is ideal. Dexter's apartment must be modest, clean, and unremarkable. A second locked room on the lot or a separate basement structure represents the kill room — plastic sheeting implied by bare walls, a single table, locked at all times except during a kill ritual.
Dexter's boat — the Slice of Life — is represented by a dock object or a separate small waterfront lot. He visits it alone at least once per week.

Dexter Morgan is a young or middle-aged adult — precise, warm on the surface, hollow underneath in ways only he can feel. Suggested traits: Genius, Neat, Loner, Good (the mask), Evil (the truth). He works a 9-to-5 schedule and must maintain it every day.

Debra Morgan is a young adult — Dexter's sister, a detective, foul-mouthed and fiercely loving. Suggested traits: Hot-Headed, Outgoing, Good, Ambitious, Romantic. She must never discover the kill room.
Harry Morgan appears only as a memory — represent him as a ghost Sim or an NPC who visits once per week and delivers one rule to Dexter before leaving. He does not stay.
Lieutenant Maria LaGuerta is a middle-aged adult — ambitious, political, closer to the truth than she knows. Suggested traits: Ambitious, Mean, Self-Assured, Snob.
Sergeant Angel Batista is a middle-aged adult — warm, perceptive, Dexter's closest work friend. Suggested traits: Good, Outgoing, Romantic, Foodie.

The Target is a rotating role — a new NPC each story arc who has committed a crime the law cannot touch. Create one per week. Suggested traits vary but must always include Evil. They must visit the lot at least once before the kill ritual.
Dexter's kill kit is represented by a locked box or cabinet in his apartment — a debug object only he may interact with. It must be checked every morning as part of his routine.

Rita Bennett is a young adult — kind, damaged, exactly the kind of normal Dexter is trying to perform. Suggested traits: Family-Oriented, Neat, Romantic, Cheerful, Good. She and her children represent Dexter's civilian anchor.
goals: Dexter must complete one kill ritual per story arc — 7 in-game days — without Debra or LaGuerta discovering the kill room.
Dexter must maintain Good Friends status with Debra at all times. If it drops below Friend, the scenario enters crisis mode.
Dexter must attend work every day — represented by leaving the apartment lot for at least 4 in-game hours during daylight.
Rita's mood must remain green for 5 out of every 7 days — she is the life Dexter is building and it must look real.
Harry must visit once per week. Dexter must interact with him before he leaves.
rules: Every morning: Dexter checks his kill kit — represented by interacting with the locked box before leaving for work. If he skips this, his Loner need spikes and he must spend one hour alone before any social interactions.
Every day: Dexter must perform the mask — at least 3 positive social interactions with non-household Sims. He is charming. He is normal. This is the work.
Every day: Dexter must eat breakfast alone before Debra wakes. This is his only truly private time. It must not be interrupted.

Every day: track two parallel need bars — Dexter's public mood (must stay green) and his Dark Passenger meter (a manual count). The Dark Passenger increases by 1 each day he does not complete a kill ritual. At 5, his Evil trait triggers and he must perform a mean interaction with someone at work.

Every 7 days: Dexter selects a Target NPC. The Target must visit the lot at least once. Dexter must gather evidence — represented by 3 separate observation or research interactions with or near the Target — before the kill ritual may proceed.

The kill ritual: move Dexter and the Target to the kill room. Lock the door. Dexter spends 2 in-game hours inside alone with the Target. The Target is removed from the household when the door opens. The Dark Passenger resets to 0.
After each kill ritual: Dexter visits the boat lot alone. He stays for at least 1 hour. This is not optional.

Debra may never enter the kill room. If her autonomous pathing takes her toward it, redirect her immediately. If she enters: the scenario enters crisis mode — Dexter must perform 5 consecutive positive interactions with her within 24 hours to reset.
LaGuerta must perform one investigative interaction per week — snooping, questioning, or observing Dexter. He must respond warmly each time.

Harry's weekly visit delivers one rule. Choose from: never kill an innocent, keep the mask on, protect Debra above all, leave no evidence, the need is not a flaw it is a tool. Each rule governs Dexter's behaviour for the following week.
If Rita's mood drops to red: Dexter must abandon all investigation activity that day and attend to her exclusively. The mask requires it.

storyBeats.text: Dexter's morning routine. Breakfast alone. The kill kit checked. The drive to Miami Metro. The colleagues who like him without knowing him. He narrates everything internally and none of it reaches his face.
A new case arrives at the department. The Target begins to take shape — someone the law has touched and released, someone Harry's Code would approve. Dexter begins to watch.
The Dark Passenger is quiet for now. Rita makes dinner. Debra calls twice. Dexter performs warmth so fluently he occasionally forgets it is a performance.
The evidence phase. Three observations. The Target does not know they are being studied. Dexter is meticulous in ways that look like professional habit.
LaGuerta is watching something at the edge of her vision. She cannot name it yet. Dexter smiles at her across the bullpen and she smiles back.
The kill room. The plastic. The ritual that makes Dexter feel, briefly and completely, like himself. The Target is removed. The boat carries what remains out past the lights of the city.
Morning. Breakfast alone. The kill kit checked. The drive to Miami Metro. The Dark Passenger is satisfied. This is sustainable. This is the Code. This is the life.

endings.title: The Code Holds
endings.text: Dexter completes 7 kill rituals across 7 story arcs without Debra or LaGuerta discovering the kill room. Rita's mood is green. Debra retains Good Friends status. The Dark Passenger never exceeds 4. Dexter stands on the boat at dawn and the city behind him is full of people who will never know what keeps them safe. The Code holds. It will hold tomorrow. It always holds until it doesn't.

endings.title: The Passenger Takes the Wheel
endings.text: The Dark Passenger reaches 5 in three consecutive weeks. Dexter's Evil trait activates permanently — remove his Good trait. He performs a kill without completing the evidence phase. The Target was not vetted. Harry does not visit this week. Dexter disposes of everything correctly but something is different now and he knows it. The scenario continues but the Code is compromised. Each subsequent week the evidence requirement drops by one observation until he is operating on instinct alone.

endings.title: Deb Knows
endings.text: Debra enters the kill room during a ritual — either by autonomous pathing or as a deliberate story choice. Her mood drops to critical. Dexter's mask interaction fails — she rejects all 5 positive interactions. She knows. The scenario ends here. What she does with what she knows is the question the series spent eight seasons answering. You decide.

title: Pretty Little Liars
description: One year after the disappearance of Alison DiLaurentis — the queen bee who knew everyone's secrets — her four best friends begin receiving threatening messages from someone who signs themselves A. A knows everything Alison knew. A might be Alison. The girls of Rosewood keep lying to protect themselves and each other, and every lie gives A more to work with. Based on the book series by Sara Shepard and the Freeform television series.
setup: Use a wealthy suburban world — Willow Creek dressed up with large lots, manicured gardens, and a visible class divide between the older family estates and the newer builds. Rosewood should feel pristine and suffocating.

Build four separate residential lots — one for each Liar — close enough that the girls can walk between them. Each house reflects its owner: Spencer's is immaculate and pressurised, Hanna's is fashionable and slightly hollow, Emily's is warm and sports-trophy-lined, Aria's is creative and cluttered with books.
A central fifth lot represents the shared social space — the Grille, the school hallway, and the park bench where messages arrive. This is where A's notes appear each morning.
Spencer Hastings is a young adult — the overachiever, the one who needs to win more than she needs to be right. Suggested traits: Perfectionist, Genius, Ambitious, Mean, Bookworm.

Hanna Marin is a young adult — the one who reinvented herself after Alison left, and is terrified of sliding back. Suggested traits: Self-Assured, Outgoing, Shopaholic (use Materialist), Romantic, Hot-Headed.
Emily Fields is a young adult — the athlete, the loyal one, the one with the most to lose from the secret A holds over her. Suggested traits: Active, Romantic, Good, Family-Oriented, Loner.
Aria Montgomery is a young adult — the creative one, the one currently making a catastrophic romantic decision. Suggested traits: Creative, Bookworm, Romantic, Outgoing, Mischievous.
Alison DiLaurentis does not begin the scenario as a playable Sim. She is represented by a locked memorial — a framed photo on the central lot. She may return as a ghost or a reveal Sim at Day 28.

Each Liar begins the scenario with one Secret — a specific behaviour she must hide from all other household Sims and NPCs. Write each secret on a note before the scenario begins and do not reveal them until A forces the issue.

A is never a playable Sim. A is a mechanic — a daily message that arrives at the central lot each morning, represented by a note object or a specific chair that one of the Liars must sit in to receive the day's punishment.
goals: All four Liars must retain at least Friend status with each other for the entire scenario — A wins when the group fractures.
Each Liar must protect her Secret for at least 14 days before it is exposed.
The group must collectively discover 5 clues about A's identity before Day 28.
Alison's fate must be resolved — either her ghost appears and delivers a clue, or a reveal Sim identified as Alison returns to the lot on Day 28.
rules: Every morning: one Liar — rotating daily in order — goes to the central lot alone and receives A's message. Roll a die to determine which punishment category applies: 1–2 = social sabotage (a friendship must be damaged), 3–4 = secret pressure (the targeted Liar must perform her secret behaviour publicly that day), 5–6 = evidence destruction (one clue already discovered is removed).
Every day: all four Liars must meet at the central lot for at least one hour. This is non-negotiable. The friendship is the only thing keeping A from winning outright.
Every day: each Liar must perform one action related to her Secret — keeping it hidden costs effort. If she does not actively maintain it, it becomes exposed the following day.

Every day: at least one NPC adult — a parent, a teacher, a detective — must interact with one of the Liars in a way that applies pressure. They are always asking the wrong questions in the right direction.
Once per week: A escalates. The punishment that week is more severe — remove a friendship, expose a secret publicly, or introduce a new NPC threat who begins investigating the group.
If any two Liars reach Enemies status: A wins a point. At 3 A points, the scenario enters endgame — A's identity must be resolved within 7 days or the group dissolves.
Clues are discovered by: completing a 2-hour research task, successfully befriending a suspicious NPC, or accessing a locked space on any of the four residential lots.
Spencer must attempt to solve A's identity independently at least once per week — she cannot help herself. If she discovers a clue without telling the others, it counts as half a clue.
Aria's romantic interest must be an NPC her parents would disapprove of. This relationship must be kept secret from her household. A knows about it from Day 1.
Hanna must perform one act of social performance per day — dressing up, attending an event, or initiating a social interaction with a popular NPC. Her reinvention requires maintenance.
Emily must perform one athletic activity per day. If she misses two consecutive days, her mood drops and A's message that morning targets her specifically.

storyBeats.text: One year after Alison. The four of them have drifted the way you drift when the person holding you together is gone. Then the first message arrives and they are together again in the worst possible way.

A knows about Spencer's plagiarism. A knows about Hanna's shoplifting. A knows about Emily. A knows about Aria's teacher. A knows everything Alison knew, which means Alison told someone, which means Alison is either alive or she told the wrong person before she died.
The first escalation. One of the secrets comes close to the surface. The girls close ranks — messily, imperfectly, in the way of people who are used to competing with each other.
A clue. It points somewhere none of them expected. Spencer pulls at it too hard and the thread leads somewhere that implicates her own family.
The group fractures slightly. A immediately applies pressure to the crack. Two of the Liars don't speak for three days. The other two hold the middle.
Five clues. The shape of A begins to emerge. Someone in Rosewood has been watching them since before Alison disappeared. Someone who knew all of them.
Day 28. The memorial lot. The photo of Alison. Something moves near it at dusk that should not be moving.

endings.title: We Know Who A Is
endings.text: The group collects 5 clues, all four Liars retain Friend status, and every Secret has been exposed but survived. On Day 28, a reveal Sim identified as Alison arrives at the central lot. She was never dead. She has been watching. She knows who A is because for a while she was running A herself, until A outgrew her. The girls stand on the central lot at night and decide together what to do with what they know. They are, despite everything, still friends.

endings.title: A Wins
endings.text: The group accumulates 3 A points — three fractured friendships. The scenario enters endgame. A's escalation that week exposes all remaining secrets simultaneously. The four Liars scatter to their separate lots. Rosewood goes quiet. The photo of Alison on the central lot is the only thing that hasn't moved. Whoever A is, they won by making the girls too afraid of each other to fight back.

endings.title: The Lie That Held
endings.text: All four Secrets are exposed within the group but never to the outside world. The Liars know everything about each other now and have chosen to stay anyway. They have 3 clues — not enough to name A — but enough to know A is someone who needed them scared more than they needed them caught. The messages stop. Not because A is gone. Because A got what they wanted.

title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
description: Buffy Summers is the Slayer — one girl in all the world, chosen to fight the vampires and the forces of darkness. She wanted to be normal. She moved to Sunnydale to start over. Sunnydale is built on a Hellmouth. There is no starting over when you are the Slayer. There is only the next thing that needs killing, and the friends strange enough to help you kill it. Based on the television series created by Joss Whedon.
setup: Use a suburban world with a clear town centre — Willow Creek works well. Sunnydale requires: a high school lot (large community build), a graveyard section (use a park lot with tombstone debug objects), a magic shop lot, and the Summers family home.

The Hellmouth is represented by a sealed basement room beneath the high school lot — locked, dark, and containing one object that pulses with ambient wrongness. It opens only during the season finale story beat.

Buffy Summers is a young adult — the Slayer, the one who wanted something else and got this instead. Suggested traits: Active, Hot-Headed, Romantic, Good, Brave (use Outgoing). She must have maximum Athletic skill at scenario start.

Willow Rosenberg is a young adult — the witch in waiting, already more powerful than she knows. Suggested traits: Genius, Bookworm, Good, Loner, Creative. She gains one level of Mischief skill per week representing her growing magical ability.
Xander Harris is a young adult — the one without powers, the one who shows up anyway. Suggested traits: Outgoing, Lazy, Romantic, Good, Clumsy (use Hot-Headed for unexpected moments of courage).

Rupert Giles is a middle-aged adult — the Watcher, the librarian, the one who knows too much and carries it badly. Suggested traits: Bookworm, Genius, Neat, Loner, Good. He must spend at least 2 hours per day in the library or magic shop.
Cordelia Chase is a young adult — mean, observant, accidentally essential. Suggested traits: Self-Assured, Mean, Outgoing, Snob, Materialist.

The Big Bad is a new Sim introduced at Day 1 — powerful, patient, operating from a lair lot at the edge of the world. Suggested traits: Evil, Mean, Genius, Self-Assured. They do not come to Buffy directly. They send minions first.
Minions are temporary NPCs — one introduced per week, removed after their encounter with Buffy. They are the Monster of the Week.

Angel is a young adult — a vampire with a soul, Buffy's catastrophic romantic decision, deeply inconvenient. Suggested traits: Romantic, Gloomy, Loner, Good, Hot-Headed. He visits at night only and must leave the lot before dawn.
goals: Buffy must defeat one Minion per week — represented by a hostile encounter ending in the Minion's removal from the lot.
The Scooby Gang — Buffy, Willow, Xander, and Giles — must retain Friend status with each other throughout.
Willow must reach Mischief level 7 before the season finale — she needs to be ready.
The Big Bad must be confronted in the Hellmouth on Day 28 or later. Buffy must enter the basement alone.
Buffy must maintain her civilian life — attend the high school lot at least 3 days per week and keep her mother Joyce's mood green.
rules: Every day: Buffy patrols the graveyard lot alone for at least 1 hour after dark. If she skips patrol, the weekly Minion gains a power boost — it takes two encounters to remove them instead of one.
Every day: the Scooby Gang meets at the library or magic shop for at least 1 hour. Research, planning, or just being together all count. The meeting must include Giles.

Every day: Buffy must perform one normal civilian activity — attending class, eating lunch with friends, having a conversation that has nothing to do with slaying. The normal life is not optional. It is what she is fighting for.
Every week: introduce one new Minion NPC. They must interact with at least one Scooby Gang member before their encounter with Buffy. This interaction is hostile.

The Minion encounter: Buffy and the Minion are placed on the graveyard lot together after dark. They must reach Enemies status before the Minion is removed. If Buffy's mood drops to red during the encounter, she requires backup — one other Scooby must arrive on the lot before the encounter resolves.

Angel may only visit after dark. He and Buffy must have one romantic interaction per visit. He must leave before 6am. If he stays past dawn: his Good trait flips to Evil for 24 hours and he must perform one hostile interaction before it resets.

Willow gains one Mischief skill level per week automatically — her powers are growing whether she trains them or not. At level 5 she may assist in Minion encounters. At level 7 she is essential for the finale.

Giles must research the Big Bad every day — represented by a 1-hour writing or research task. Each completed session adds one point to the Watcher's Knowledge meter. At 7 points, the Hellmouth location is confirmed and the finale may begin.

Cordelia must perform one mean interaction per day — she cannot help it — but must also perform one moment of unexpected usefulness per week. She saves someone's life by accident at least once per arc.

Joyce Summers is an NPC who visits the Summers home. Her mood must remain green. She must never learn about slaying. If she asks where Buffy goes at night, Buffy must perform a successful Charisma interaction to deflect her.
If any Scooby Gang member's mood reaches critical red: they are in danger. Move them off the main lot immediately and have Buffy retrieve them within 24 in-game hours.

storyBeats.text: Buffy arrives in Sunnydale hoping this time will be different. Giles finds her in the library on the first day. He has been expecting her. She did not want to be expected.
The first Minion. Willow and Xander see something they cannot explain. They do not run. This is the beginning of the Scooby Gang even if no one has named it yet.
Angel. He appears at night with warnings and terrible timing. Buffy knows she should not. She does anyway. Giles looks at them both with the expression of a man watching a structural problem develop.
The Big Bad's influence begins to reach into the school. A teacher behaves wrongly. A student disappears. The Hellmouth hums beneath the library floor.
Willow's power surprises everyone, including Willow. Xander does something brave in a way he will never be given full credit for. Cordelia saves someone's life and is furious about it.
Giles reaches 7 points of Watcher's Knowledge. The Hellmouth basement unlocks. The Big Bad has been waiting. They have always been waiting. Sunnydale was always going to come to this.
The season finale. The Hellmouth. Buffy goes in alone because that is what the Slayer does. The Gang is outside because that is what the Gang does. The door closes.

endings.title: She Saved the World a Lot
endings.text: Buffy enters the Hellmouth alone on Day 28 or later. Willow is at level 7 Mischief. Giles has 7 Watcher points. The Big Bad is removed from the lot after a 2-hour encounter. Buffy emerges. The Gang is there. Cordelia says something cutting that is also secretly kind. Angel stands slightly apart in the dark. Sunnydale is safe until the next thing. There is always a next thing. Buffy goes home and eats cereal at the kitchen counter and her mother asks how school was.

endings.title: The Cost
endings.text: Buffy defeats the Big Bad but one Scooby Gang member's mood is critical red at the end of the finale. They survive but are removed from the household for the following arc. Buffy stands outside the Hellmouth after and the victory feels correct and terrible in equal measure. Willow cries. Xander holds it together until he is alone. Giles cleans his glasses for a very long time.

endings.title: The Chosen One Falls
endings.text: Buffy enters the Hellmouth but Willow has not reached level 7 and Giles has fewer than 5 Watcher points. The encounter cannot be completed. Buffy is removed from the lot. The Gang stands outside the sealed basement. In every generation there is a Chosen One. She alone will face the vampires and the forces of darkness. She alone. Until the next one.

title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
description: On her sixteenth birthday, Sabrina Spellman discovers she is a witch. Her aunts Hilda and Zelda have been waiting to tell her. Her cat Salem has opinions about everything. Westbridge is full of mortals who must never find out. Harvey is her boyfriend and knows nothing. Libby is her nemesis and suspects nothing. Sabrina just wants to get through high school without accidentally turning anyone into a frog. Based on the Archie Comics character and the ABC/WB television series.
setup: Use a cosy suburban world — Henford-on-Bagley or Willow Creek. The Spellman house must be large, slightly eccentric, and full of peculiar objects. A dedicated magic practice room — the linen closet — must exist as a small locked room accessible only to Sabrina, Hilda, and Zelda. It contains a spellbook object and a cauldron.
The Other Realm is represented by a mirror object in the linen closet. Sabrina may interact with it once per week to receive magical guidance — this counts as her weekly lesson.

Sabrina Spellman is a teenager — curious, impulsive, and catastrophically well-intentioned. Suggested traits: Creative, Outgoing, Good, Clumsy (use Hot-Headed for accidental spell misfires), Bookworm. She begins with Mischief skill level 1 representing her raw untrained magic.
Hilda Spellman is a middle-aged adult — chaotic, warm, the aunt who says yes when Zelda says no. Suggested traits: Outgoing, Mischievous, Romantic, Creative, Hot-Headed.
Zelda Spellman is a middle-aged adult — brilliant, principled, secretly softer than she presents. Suggested traits: Genius, Perfectionist, Bookworm, Snob, Good.

Salem Saberhagen is represented by a cat in the household. He is the most important member of the family and must always have green needs. He was a warlock turned into a cat as punishment for attempted world domination. He has not entirely given up on the world domination.

Harvey Kinkle is a young adult — sweet, uncomplicated, exactly what Sabrina needs and exactly what makes magic complicated. Suggested traits: Good, Outgoing, Romantic, Cheerful, Lazy. He must never witness a successful spell.
Libby Chessler is a young adult — the social queen of Westbridge High, Sabrina's primary mortal obstacle. Suggested traits: Mean, Self-Assured, Outgoing, Snob, Materialist.
The Witch's Council is represented by a single NPC elder who visits once per week to assess Sabrina's progress. They are never satisfied but never quite unsatisfied enough to intervene.
goals: Sabrina must reach Mischief level 5 — her magic is becoming reliable enough to control.

Sabrina must pass her Witch's License test — represented by completing 3 consecutive days with no accidental spell misfires and a visit from the Witch's Council elder ending in a positive interaction.
Harvey must never witness a successful spell. He may witness the aftermath of one — a misfired result — but Sabrina must successfully lie her way out of it using Charisma.
Sabrina must maintain Friend status with at least 3 mortal classmates — she is living both lives and both must work.
rules: Every day: Sabrina practices magic in the linen closet for at least 1 hour. If she skips practice, she loses one Mischief skill point and must explain a magical mishap to Zelda.
Every day: Sabrina attends the school lot for at least 3 hours. Her mortal life requires maintenance — grades, friendships, the performance of being normal.
Every day: Salem must be fed and have his needs attended to before Sabrina leaves the house. He will not remind her politely.
Every day: Hilda and Zelda must disagree about something — one supportive interaction between them must be preceded by one argument. This is just how they are.

Once per week: Sabrina attempts a spell outside the linen closet. Roll a die — on a 1 or 2, the spell misfires. A misfire means one of the following occurs: an NPC is temporarily changed (move them off the lot for 24 hours and return them), an object breaks, or Sabrina's mood drops to red.
If Harvey is present when a spell misfires: Sabrina must perform a successful Charisma interaction within 1 hour to explain it away. If she fails, Harvey's relationship score drops by one tier.

Once per week: the Witch's Council elder visits. Sabrina must perform 2 positive interactions with them. If she performs one hostile or failed interaction, the Witch's License test is delayed by one week.

Libby must perform one act of social sabotage per day — a mean interaction, a public humiliation, or an attempt to separate Sabrina from her mortal friends. Sabrina may retaliate once per week using magic, but only if no mortals are watching.

Zelda conducts a weekly magic assessment — Sabrina must demonstrate her current Mischief skill level to Zelda. If her level has not increased that week, Zelda assigns additional practice hours and Sabrina loses one free afternoon.
Hilda must provide one piece of terrible magical advice per week that Sabrina follows with predictable consequences. The consequences are always recoverable. Barely.

storyBeats.text: Sabrina's birthday. The aunts sit her down. Salem jumps on the table. The linen closet glows. Everything Sabrina thought she knew about herself is correct — she always felt different — and also completely upended.
The first spell outside the closet. It almost works. Harvey is in the next room. Zelda appears from nowhere with the expression of someone who has been bracing for this for sixteen years.
Libby does something cruel at school. Sabrina's hands feel warm. Hilda says she should use the magic. Zelda says absolutely not. Sabrina uses the magic. It does not go as planned.
The Witch's Council elder arrives for the first time. They look at Sabrina the way customs officials look at luggage they are certain is hiding something.
Harvey almost sees something he should not. Sabrina's Charisma saves them both. He looks at her with total trust and she feels terrible about it in a way that is also very sweet.

The Witch's License test approaches. Sabrina has three consecutive clean days ahead of her. Hilda suggests celebrating. Zelda suggests absolutely not celebrating. Salem suggests a plan that would benefit Salem specifically.
The Council elder returns. The test. Sabrina's hands are steady. The magic does what she tells it to. She is a witch. She has always been a witch. Now she can prove it.

endings.title: License Granted
endings.text: Sabrina reaches Mischief level 5, completes 3 consecutive misfire-free days, and passes the Council elder's assessment. Harvey never witnessed a successful spell. She has 3 mortal friends and a cat who is prouder of her than he will ever admit out loud. The linen closet glows a warmer colour than usual. Zelda allows herself one small smile. Hilda throws a party that gets slightly out of hand. Salem begins quietly revising his world domination timeline.

endings.title: Harvey Finds Out
endings.text: Harvey witnesses a successful spell and Sabrina's Charisma fails to explain it. His relationship score drops to Acquaintance. Sabrina must spend the following 7 days with no magic use at all — she is trying to be just a girl for him. If his score recovers to Friend by the end of the week, the scenario continues. If it does not, Harvey leaves the household. The Witch's Council elder notes this in their file with visible satisfaction.

endings.title: The Other Realm Calls
endings.text: Sabrina reaches Mischief level 5 but fails the Witch's License test three times. The Council elder informs her she must spend one week in the Other Realm for intensive training — represented by removing Sabrina from the lot for 7 days. Harvey waits. Libby fills the social vacuum immediately. When Sabrina returns she is more powerful and has missed something she cannot get back. The license test is rescheduled. She passes on the first attempt.

title: The Others
description: Grace Stewart waits in her fog-bound wartime manor for her husband to return from the front, protecting her two photosensitive children with religious ferocity. Three new servants arrive. Strange things begin to happen. Grace is certain the house is haunted. She is not wrong — but she is not right, either. Written and directed by Alejandro Amenábar.
setup: Build or download a large, dark Victorian manor surrounded by fog — Moonwood Mill or a custom Windenburg lot. All windows must be covered with heavy curtains or blocked by shrubs. No natural light should reach the interior.
The house must have a sealed room — locked, curtained, never redecorated. This is the children's former nursery.
Grace Stewart is a middle-aged adult — devout, controlling, exhausted. Suggested traits: Neat, Mean, Gloomy, Paranoid (use Erratic), Family-Oriented. She begins with no outside friendships.

Anne and Nicholas are child Sims — Anne is older, curious, and prone to telling her mother uncomfortable truths. Nicholas is younger and more fearful. Both have the Gloomy trait and must remain indoors at all times.
The three servants — Mrs Mills, Mr Tuttle, and Lydia — are older adults. Suggested traits: Gloomy, Loner, Perfectionist. They arrived without references. They never discuss where they came from.
The house must remain dark — use the lowest lighting settings possible throughout. Candles and low-wattage lamps only.
No other Sims should visit the lot. Any NPC who arrives uninvited must be immediately turned away by Grace.
goals: Grace must maintain green mood for all three children and servants simultaneously for 7 consecutive days — her control holds.
Anne must trigger a 'truth event' — she must initiate a conversation with Grace that results in a negative moodlet or tense interaction.
Grace must discover the servants' secret — represented by finding the sealed room unlocked on Day 21.
rules: Every day: all curtains and windows must remain blocked. If any Sim autonomously opens a curtain, Grace must immediately close it and scold that Sim.
Every day: Grace leads the children in a structured lesson or religious reading — represented by a 1-hour period of no autonomous actions for the children.
Every day: Anne must attempt one act of minor defiance — opening a curtain, leaving her room, or speaking to a servant without Grace's knowledge.
Once per week: a 'presence event' occurs — a door opens by itself, an object moves, or Anne reports seeing a figure in the curtained room. Grace must investigate and find nothing.
Grace may not leave the lot under any circumstances. She is waiting for her husband to return.
If Grace's mood drops to red for 2 consecutive days: she locks the children in their room for 24 hours and does not interact with the servants.

storyBeats.text: The servants arrive on a foggy morning with no references and no explanation. Grace hires them anyway — the house is too large to manage alone.
Anne tells her mother she has seen a boy in the house. Grace dismisses it. The servants exchange a glance she does not see.
The presences multiply. Curtains open by themselves. Voices come from sealed rooms. Grace's control of the household begins to cost her more than it gives.
Charles Stewart returns from the front — changed, distant, wrong. He does not stay. His departure is worse than his absence.
Grace confronts the servants. Mrs Mills speaks carefully. What she says does not make sense — until suddenly, horribly, it does.
The sealed room opens. Anne was telling the truth all along. Grace has always known, somewhere, what the truth was.

endings.title: This House Is Ours
endings.text: Grace reaches Day 21. The sealed room opens. Grace enters and finds it furnished as the servants left it — not as she arranged it. She understands. She gathers the children. She tells them they may never leave, but she says it more gently than before. The servants stay. They have nowhere else to go. The manor endures.

endings.title: The Husband Returns
endings.text: If a male Sim is added to the household on Day 14 and then removed again within 48 hours: this represents Charles's brief return. If Grace's mood is green when he departs: she accepts her situation with something close to peace. The house settles.

endings.title: The Intruders
endings.text: An NPC successfully enters the lot and encounters Grace. Grace's erratic trait triggers — she attacks or drives them off. The NPC calls for help. Officials arrive. The scenario ends with outsiders inside Manderley — and Grace, finally, having to explain herself.

title: Hereditary
description: When Ellen Graham dies, her daughter Annie begins to unravel the disturbing secrets her mother kept hidden. As grief spreads through the family like an infection, something older and more deliberate moves in behind it. Written and directed by Ari Aster.
setup: Use a large isolated house on a rural lot — Henford-on-Bagley is ideal. A treehouse or separate backyard structure represents the grandmother's private space and later the cult's gathering place.

Annie Graham is a middle-aged adult — an artist, precise and controlled, with grief she has not yet located. Suggested traits: Creative, Perfectionist, Gloomy, Erratic, Bookworm. She works from a home studio.

Steve Graham is a middle-aged adult — steady, patient, and fatally passive. Suggested traits: Good, Neat, Family-Oriented, Cheerful. He is the household's emotional ballast and the scenario suffers when he is gone.
Peter Graham is a teenage Sim — detached, guilty, slowly being hollowed out. Suggested traits: Loner, Lazy, Gloomy, Erratic.
Charlie Graham is a child Sim — strange, watchful, allergic. Suggested traits: Loner, Gloomy, Creative. She must carry a nut allergy flag — no nut-containing foods may be served to her.
Ellen Graham does not appear as a playable Sim. She is represented by a locked attic room filled with her belongings, photos, and a single shrine object. This room unlocks on Day 3.
Annie's miniature workshop must contain small doll-house builds that mirror the family home exactly — use debug objects and miniature furniture to replicate each room of the lot.
A second structure on the lot — the treehouse or shed — must be locked until Day 14. It contains occult objects, cult symbols, and a copy of the same photo that appears in Ellen's attic.
goals: Annie must complete three miniature builds in her workshop — one for each major story beat — before the scenario ends.
Steve must retain Good Friends status with Annie until the moment he cannot.
Peter must reach rock-bottom mood — red in every need category simultaneously — before the final story beat triggers.
Charlie must be removed from the household by Day 7. How this happens is your choice — but it must happen.
rules: Every day: Annie spends at least 2 hours in her workshop. If she does not, her Erratic trait triggers — she must perform one hostile interaction with a family member.
Every day: Charlie performs one strange autonomous behaviour — clicking her tongue (represented by a repetitive idle animation), drawing alone, or eating something she should not.
Charlie must never attend a social event or leave the lot with more than one other Sim present.
Once Charlie is removed from the household on Day 7: Peter's mood immediately drops to red and may not recover above yellow for the remainder of the scenario.
Every day after Day 7: Annie must attempt one interaction with Peter. He must reject it — use mean or dismissive responses only.
From Day 14 onward: Annie begins attending a grief support group — represented by a weekly off-lot visit. Joan, an NPC she meets there, must be invited to the lot at least once.
Joan must never enter the house. She may only interact with Annie in the garden or on the lot boundary.
Once Joan visits: Annie begins performing a ritual in the backyard each night. Represent this by having Annie stand outside alone for one in-game hour after dark.

storyBeats.text: Ellen Graham is buried. The family returns to the house. Annie begins a new miniature — a recreation of the funeral. Charlie watches from the doorway and makes her sound.
The attic unlocks. Annie finds her mother's things — photos, letters, names she does not recognise. The treehouse at the back of the lot catches her eye.
Charlie is removed from the household. The manner of it is sudden and wrong. Peter drives home alone. Annie does not sleep.
The grief support group. Joan is kind and specific in ways that feel rehearsed. She teaches Annie something she should not have learned.
Annie finds the treehouse unlocked. Inside: her mother's work, the cult's marks, and the realisation that grief was never the point. The ritual was.
Peter's body is no longer entirely Peter's. Annie moves through the house above him. Steve stands between them for as long as he can.

endings.title: Reigning
endings.text: Peter's mood reaches critical red across all needs. Steve is removed from the household by any means. Annie completes her third miniature — a replica of the treehouse. Move Annie to the treehouse permanently and lock the main house. Peter remains inside alone. The cult has what it came for. Paimon is seated.

endings.title: The Notebook
endings.text: Steve discovers Annie's ritual notebook before Day 21. His mood drops to red. He burns it — remove the occult objects from the lot. Annie's erratic trait triggers into full breakdown. The ritual is interrupted. The family does not survive intact, but Paimon does not get his throne. The cost is everything anyway.

endings.title: The Miniature
endings.text: Annie completes all three miniatures but Steve remains in the household with green mood to the end. The ritual is never completed. The scenario ends with the family fractured but present — living inside a house that is already, in every way that matters, a doll's house.

title: Midsommar
description: Dani Ardor travels to Sweden with her emotionally distant boyfriend Christian and his friends to attend a midsummer festival at a rural commune. The commune is beautiful, generous, and entirely closed. The festival runs for ninety days. Not everyone is meant to leave. Written and directed by Ari Aster.
setup: Build or download a bright, open Scandinavian commune — flower arches, communal dining tables, a large decorated Maypole, and several small sleeping cottages. Snowy worlds repurposed with spring/summer colour work well. Everything should be excessively cheerful.
One building on the commune lot must be locked and curtained — the Ritual Temple. It unlocks at Day 21.
Dani Ardor is a young adult — recently bereaved, emotionally dependent, and far stronger than she knows. Suggested traits: Gloomy, Romantic, Bookworm, Emotional (use Erratic), Family-Oriented.

Christian Hughes is a young adult — handsome, non-committal, and coasting. Suggested traits: Romantic, Lazy, Self-Assured, Noncommittal (use Loner). He should not initiate affectionate interactions with Dani.
Josh is a young adult academic — serious, opportunistic, here for his thesis. Suggested traits: Bookworm, Genius, Ambitious, Loner.
Mark is a young adult — crass, impatient, and magnificently unsuited for the situation. Suggested traits: Outgoing, Hot-Headed, Lazy, Mischievous.

Pelle is a young adult commune member — warm, genuine, and the reason any of them are here. Suggested traits: Outgoing, Cheerful, Romantic, Mischievous. He is the only commune member the visitors fully trust.
Add 6–8 commune NPCs as household members or frequent visitors. They must always be in groups — no commune member should be alone with a visitor unless a ritual event triggers.
All visitors begin the scenario in the same sleeping cottage. The cottage has no locks on the inside.
goals: Dani must be crowned May Queen — reach the highest Charisma score among all female Sims on the lot by Day 14.
Christian, Josh, and Mark must each be removed from the household before Day 28. The order and method are your choice.
Dani must end the scenario with green mood and a new Best Friend among the commune members.
rules: Every day: the commune provides all meals. Visitors may not cook for themselves.
Every day: at least one commune ritual must be performed — represented by all Sims on the lot gathering at the Maypole or communal table for one hour.
Every day: Christian must fail to comfort Dani at least once — if her mood drops, he must be directed away rather than toward her.
Every day: Pelle must perform one warm, attentive interaction with Dani that Christian does not.
Once per week: a visitor is removed from the household. The commune members' moods all improve when this happens.
Mark must be the first visitor removed — on or before Day 7 — due to an act of disrespect on the commune grounds.
Josh must be removed between Day 7 and Day 14 — caught where he should not be, taking what was not offered.
Christian must be removed last — on or before Day 21 — in a manner befitting the Maypole.
After each removal: Dani's relationship with the remaining commune members improves by one tier.
Dani may not leave the lot at any point. She chose to stay — even when she could have gone.

storyBeats.text: The commune greets them with flowers and open arms. The food is extraordinary. The light never quite fades. Dani cries once, privately, and Pelle is the only one who notices.
The first ritual. It is not what any of them expected. Mark laughs. The commune does not.
